title: Long Occlusion — Target Re-Acquisition
description: SECURITY BREACH: A high-risk target was observed entering a large shipping container area on CAM-01, then disappeared into occlusion. The agent must track the predicted exit vector, switch to CAM-02 to re-acquire the target emerging from the container, confirm the threat has persisted, and escalate.

frames.description: Warehouse asset area. A person in dark clothing is observed moving rapidly toward large shipping containers near the far right of the frame. No badge visible.
Warehouse asset area. The person is entering the gap between containers — entering occlusion phase. Tracking is lost in this camera.
Warehouse asset area. Target fully occluded behind containers. No visible movement. Camera coverage ends here.
Shipping container exit corridor. Empty. This is the predicted exit route based on container layout.

Shipping container exit corridor. TARGET RE-ACQUIRED: The same person in dark clothing is emerging from between containers on the left side. Persistence confirmed — this is the same individual from CAM-01.
Shipping container exit corridor. Subject is now moving toward the restricted warehouse exit carrying a bag that appears heavier than when they entered. Active threat confirmed.
Shipping container exit corridor. Subject approaching the loading dock exit. Urgency increasing — potential escape imminent.
External loading dock. No unusual vehicles or persons detected at this time.
External loading dock. An unmarked vehicle has appeared at the loading dock — possibly a getaway vehicle coordinating with the subject inside.
